summary: Comprehensive testing of GPS enforcement feature. All 9 backend tests passed. Frontend location prompt UI verified for both 'Not Here' and 'Here & Now' modes.

tests: /api/discovery/not-here returns 400 when user has no lat/lng coordinates
/api/discovery/here returns 400 when user has no lat/lng coordinates
Discovery works after coordinates are updated via /location/update
Demo user with coordinates can access discovery endpoints
Location update validates coordinate values (rejects missing lat/lng)

ui_elements: MapPinOff icon displayed when location denied
'Location access denied. Please enable location in your browser settings to see nearby people.' message
Privacy message: 'Your exact location is used only for distance matching and is never shared publicly.'
Radius selector (0-10 miles, 10-25 miles) visible in Not Here mode

critical_code_review_comments: Backend discovery.py correctly checks for lat/lng and returns 400 with 'Location required. Please enable GPS to see nearby users.' message
Backend venues.py /location/update endpoint correctly saves lat, lng, location_updated_at, and last_active_at
Frontend Discovery.js correctly implements location request flow with requestAndUpdateLocation() function
Frontend shows appropriate location prompt UI when geolocation is denied or unavailable
data-testid attributes present for key UI elements: request-location-btn, request-location-btn-here, select-here-now, select-not-here, back-to-discovery, radius-0-10, radius-10-25
seed_data_creation: Created test user without coordinates for GPS enforcement testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: GPS enforcement feature fully implemented and tested. Backend returns 400 when user has no lat/lng coordinates for discovery endpoints. Frontend shows 'Location Required' prompt with 'Enable Location' button when geolocation is denied. The /api/location/update endpoint correctly saves user coordinates. Demo user has coordinates stored (51.5074, -0.1278) for London.
